skill_library.name: CRM Administration & Architecture
skill_library.description: Ability to administer and architect CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot) including data models, workflows, lead routing, user permissions, and integrations with third-party tools. Includes maintaining data hygiene and serving as system administrator.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can navigate CRM, create basic reports, and manage user records
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can configure workflows, build custom fields/objects, manage integrations, and maintain data quality
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect complex CRM environments, design scalable data models, implement CPQ, and lead system migrations

skill_library.name: Pipeline & Forecasting Management
skill_library.description: Ability to build and manage sales pipeline infrastructure including deal stages, forecasting models, pipeline hygiene, coverage models, and revenue predictability frameworks. Ensures leadership has accurate visibility into future revenue.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can track pipeline in CRM and generate basic pipeline reports
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can build forecasting models, identify pipeline gaps, and implement coverage frameworks
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can design multi-segment forecasting systems, build predictive models, and drive pipeline strategy across regions

skill_library.name: GTM Process Design & Optimization
skill_library.description: Ability to design, document, and optimize go-to-market processes across the customer lifecycle — from lead generation through renewal. Includes lead-to-cash process mapping, handoff design, and funnel optimization.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic GTM process components and can document existing workflows
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can identify bottlenecks, redesign processes, and implement improvements across pre-sale and post-sale stages
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect end-to-end GTM operating models, design scalable processes for new markets, and drive cross-functional GTM transformation

skill_library.name: Commercial Analytics & Revenue Insights
skill_library.description: Ability to analyze commercial data including GMV, revenue, cohort performance, sales velocity, churn, and conversion metrics. Turns raw data into actionable narratives for executives and customer-facing teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can pull and present basic revenue and pipeline reports
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can build cohort analyses, identify trends, and create executive dashboards with actionable insights
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can design comprehensive commercial analytics frameworks, build predictive revenue models, and drive data strategy for the commercial org

skill_library.name: GTM Tech Stack Integration
skill_library.description: Ability to evaluate, implement, and integrate GTM tools across the sales and marketing technology stack — CRM, engagement platforms, enrichment tools, analytics, and productivity tools. Ensures minimal data friction and maximum adoption.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use and configure individual GTM tools (Gong, ZoomInfo, etc.)
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design integrations between CRM and third-party tools, manage data flows, and troubleshoot issues
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect the full GTM tech stack, evaluate and select vendors, and drive organization-wide adoption strategies

skill_library.name: OKR & Goal Framework Management
skill_library.description: Ability to design, implement, and manage OKR (Objectives and Key Results) or similar goal-setting frameworks. Ensures alignment between company strategy and team-level execution through structured planning cycles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands OKR methodology and can track team-level OKRs
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can facilitate OKR planning sessions, identify misalignment, and drive quarterly review cycles
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect company-wide OKR systems, integrate with business reviews, and drive strategic alignment across all functions

skill_library.name: Executive Operating Rhythm Design
skill_library.description: Ability to structure and manage the cadence of executive meetings, business reviews, planning cycles, and decision-making forums. Ensures leadership discussions are focused on decisions and outcomes rather than status updates.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can prepare meeting agendas and track action items from leadership meetings
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can structure MBR/QBR formats, manage leadership cadence, and drive accountability on decisions
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can design full executive operating rhythms including annual planning, board prep, offsites, and strategic review frameworks

skill_library.name: Business Case & Financial Modeling
skill_library.description: Ability to build business cases, financial models, and operational roadmaps to support strategic decision-making. Includes market entry analysis, M&A valuation, headcount modeling, and ROI frameworks.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build basic financial models and ROI calculations in Excel
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can develop comprehensive business cases with scenario analysis, market sizing, and operational roadmaps
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can build complex M&A valuation models, lead strategic planning processes, and present investment cases to board and investors

skill_library.name: Cross-Functional Program Execution
skill_library.description: Ability to drive complex initiatives that span multiple departments without formal authority. Includes identifying dependencies, flagging risks, resolving bottlenecks, and ensuring aligned execution across Product, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Engineering.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can coordinate tasks across 2-3 teams and track progress
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can lead multi-team initiatives, manage stakeholder expectations, and resolve cross-functional conflicts
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can drive company-wide transformational initiatives, navigate complex organizational dynamics, and influence senior leaders without direct authority

skill_library.name: Executive Communication & Decision Support
skill_library.description: Ability to synthesize complex information into clear, actionable insights for senior leadership. Includes structuring executive briefings, preparing board materials, and translating strategy into digestible communication for different audiences.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can prepare clear summaries and meeting notes for leadership
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can structure executive presentations, write CEO communications, and synthesize cross-functional updates
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can serve as an executive proxy, manage board-level communications, and act as a thought partner to C-suite on complex strategic decisions

skill_library.name: Business Process Automation
skill_library.description: Ability to identify manual processes and implement automation solutions using workflow tools, AI agents, and intelligent systems. Includes designing automated workflows, implementing AI-driven processes, and driving adoption of automation across teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can identify automation opportunities and implement basic workflow automations
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design and implement multi-step automated workflows, integrate AI tools, and measure automation ROI
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect organization-wide automation strategies, implement AI agents and intelligent systems, and drive cultural adoption of automation

skill_library.name: Enablement & Training Delivery
skill_library.description: Ability to design and deliver training programs for new tools, processes, and workflows. Includes creating documentation, running enablement sessions, tracking adoption metrics, and ensuring operational readiness for new initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic training materials and documentation for new processes
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design and deliver comprehensive enablement programs, build playbooks, and track adoption
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can build scalable enablement frameworks across global teams, systematize knowledge management, and drive organization-wide readiness for change

skill_library.name: Operational Scaling & Global Operations
skill_library.description: Ability to build scalable operational processes that support company growth across multiple regions, products, and teams. Includes managing offshore teams, standardizing operations across geographies, and designing frameworks for operational consistency at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow and execute established operational processes across teams
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can adapt processes for new markets or products and coordinate across time zones
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can build global operational infrastructure from scratch, manage distributed teams, and design scalable frameworks for multi-region execution

skill_library.name: Strategic Thinking
skill_library.description: Ability to think at a high level about business direction, market dynamics, and long-term positioning. Includes synthesizing complex information, identifying patterns and opportunities, and connecting operational decisions to strategic outcomes.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can understand and articulate company strategy and connect daily work to strategic goals
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can identify strategic opportunities, frame trade-offs, and contribute to strategic planning processes
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can develop and drive company-level strategy, anticipate market shifts, and make high-stakes strategic decisions

skill_library.name: Attention to Detail & Accuracy
skill_library.description: Ability to maintain high accuracy and thoroughness in work output. Includes spotting errors in data, documents, and processes, maintaining quality standards, and ensuring precision in execution across complex workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow checklists and catch obvious errors in own work
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design quality checks, spot subtle inconsistencies, and maintain accuracy across multiple workstreams
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can build quality assurance frameworks, establish accuracy standards for teams, and maintain precision under high-pressure, fast-paced conditions

skill_library.name: Emotional Intelligence & Interpersonal Judgment
skill_library.description: Ability to perceive, understand, and manage emotions in oneself and others. Includes reading organizational dynamics, navigating sensitive situations, building trust across levels, and influencing without authority through empathy and social awareness.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can maintain professional composure and build positive working relationships
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can navigate sensitive interpersonal dynamics, mediate conflicts, and build trust across teams and functions
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can read and influence complex organizational dynamics, manage high-stakes relationships with discretion, and serve as a trusted advisor to senior executives

skill_library.name: Sales Compensation & Incentive Design
skill_library.description: Ability to design and manage sales compensation plans that drive the right behaviors. Includes quota setting, commission modeling, SPIFs, accelerators, and territory design. Ensures comp plans align seller incentives with company revenue goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic comp plan structures and can administer commission tracking in a spreadsheet or CRM
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design role-specific comp plans, model quota scenarios, and manage SPIFs and accelerator tiers
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can architect company-wide compensation frameworks, run comp benchmarking, and align incentive design with multi-segment GTM strategy

skill_library.name: Consulting & Structured Problem Solving
skill_library.description: Ability to apply structured frameworks to diagnose and solve complex business problems. Includes issue tree decomposition, MECE structuring, hypothesis-driven analysis, and slide-based communication of findings. Common in ex-McKinsey, BCG, and Bain hires.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can apply basic problem-solving frameworks and structure problems into components
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can independently run hypothesis-driven analyses, build MECE frameworks, and communicate findings to senior stakeholders
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can lead complex diagnostic engagements, synthesize ambiguous business problems into actionable strategies, and drive structured decision-making at the executive level

skill_library.name: Domain & Industry Expertise
skill_library.description: Deep knowledge of a specific industry vertical (e.g. logistics, fintech, SaaS, gaming, healthtech) that enables better operational and strategic decisions. Allows faster onboarding, more credible stakeholder conversations, and stronger contextual judgment.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Has foundational knowledge of a specific industry and can apply it in day-to-day work
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Understands industry dynamics, key players, business models, and regulatory context well enough to advise internal stakeholders
proficiency_levels.advanced: Recognized as a subject matter expert in the domain — can drive strategy, represent the company externally, and make high-quality decisions based on deep contextual knowledge

skill_library.name: AI Tool Fluency
skill_library.description: Ability to use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, AI agents, and similar platforms) to streamline workflows, generate insights, and automate repetitive tasks. Increasingly listed as a must-have in Israeli tech job listings across operations, product, and business roles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use AI tools for basic tasks like drafting, summarizing, and research
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design AI-assisted workflows, use AI for data analysis and content generation, and integrate AI tools into team processes
proficiency_levels.advanced: Can build and deploy AI agents, design multi-step AI workflows, and drive organization-wide adoption of AI tooling to meaningfully reduce manual work

skill_library.name: Programming Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Core programming knowledge including syntax, control flow, functions, OOP, error handling, and writing clean, readable code across one or more languages.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can write basic scripts, understand variables, loops, conditionals, and functions. Follows existing code patterns.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Writes well-structured, maintainable code using OOP principles. Understands design patterns, error handling, and testing basics.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Writes idiomatic, performant code across multiple languages. Deep understanding of language internals, memory models, and compilation/interpretation.

skill_library.name: Python Development
skill_library.description: Professional Python development for production systems, including frameworks, package management, type hints, testing, async programming, and Python-specific tooling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes functional Python scripts. Familiar with standard library, pip, and basic data structures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production services using frameworks (FastAPI, Django, Flask). Uses type hints, pytest, virtual environments, and async patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large Python codebases with advanced patterns (decorators, metaclasses, generators). Expert in performance profiling, C extensions, and Python internals.

skill_library.name: Backend Development
skill_library.description: Building server-side applications, APIs, and services. Includes web frameworks, database integration, authentication, caching, message queues, and production deployment.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build simple REST APIs with a framework. Understands request/response cycle, basic authentication, and database queries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and builds production-grade backend services with proper error handling, logging, caching, and security. Experienced with microservices patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex backend systems handling high throughput. Expert in distributed patterns, event-driven architectures, and performance optimization at scale.

skill_library.name: Frontend Development
skill_library.description: Building user-facing web applications using modern frameworks, component-based architecture, state management, responsive design, and browser APIs.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build basic web pages with HTML/CSS/JavaScript. Familiar with a modern framework (React, Vue, or Angular) at a basic level.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex UIs with component architecture, state management, routing, and API integration. Understands responsive design and accessibility.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large frontend applications with performance optimization, custom hooks/composables, design systems, and deep browser performance tuning.

skill_library.name: System Design Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Understanding of how software systems are structured, including client-server architecture, databases, APIs, caching, load balancing, and basic scalability concepts.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands client-server model, basic database design, and REST API structure.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Can design simple distributed systems with appropriate database choices, caching layers, and API contracts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs multi-component systems with proper trade-off analysis between consistency, availability, and partition tolerance.

skill_library.name: System Design & Architecture
skill_library.description: Advanced system design including distributed architectures, microservices, event-driven systems, database selection, caching strategies, load balancing, and scalability planning.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can articulate trade-offs between monolith and microservices. Understands basic distributed system challenges.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs scalable systems with appropriate patterns (event-driven, CQRS, pub/sub). Makes informed database and infrastructure choices.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests. Expert in consistency models, fault tolerance, and multi-region deployment strategies.

skill_library.name: System Architecture & Vision
skill_library.description: Defining long-term architectural vision for product domains, anticipating scaling bottlenecks, designing resilient distributed systems, and making strategic technology decisions that support 10x growth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can evaluate existing architectures and identify improvement areas. Understands architectural patterns and their trade-offs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Defines architectural roadmaps for product domains. Leads architectural migrations and modernization efforts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets enterprise-wide architectural vision. Anticipates scaling needs years ahead. Drives platform-level architectural decisions affecting multiple product lines.

skill_library.name: Distributed Systems
skill_library.description: Design and operation of systems distributed across multiple nodes, including consistency models, replication, partitioning, consensus protocols, and fault tolerance.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands CAP theorem, basic replication, and partitioning concepts. Can work with distributed databases and message queues.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs systems with appropriate consistency guarantees. Experienced with distributed caching, event streaming (Kafka), and service discovery.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects highly available, globally distributed systems. Expert in consensus protocols, eventual consistency patterns, and distributed transaction management.

skill_library.name: Git & Version Control
skill_library.description: Using Git for version control, branching strategies, pull requests, code review workflows, and collaborative development practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can clone, commit, push, pull, and create branches. Understands basic merge and pull request workflows.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses advanced branching strategies (GitFlow, trunk-based). Comfortable with rebasing, cherry-picking, and resolving complex merge conflicts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines Git workflows for teams. Expert in repository management, CI/CD integration, monorepo strategies, and Git internals.

skill_library.name: Debugging & Troubleshooting
skill_library.description: Systematic approaches to identifying and resolving software defects, including debugging tools, logging analysis, root cause analysis, and troubleshooting production issues.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use debuggers, print statements, and basic logging to identify simple bugs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Debugs complex multi-component issues using profiling tools, log aggregation, and systematic hypothesis testing.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert at diagnosing distributed system failures, race conditions, memory leaks, and production incidents under pressure.

skill_library.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
skill_library.description: Knowledge of fundamental data structures (arrays, trees, graphs, hash maps) and algorithms (sorting, searching, dynamic programming) including complexity analysis.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic data structures and their use cases. Can analyze simple time/space complexity.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Selects appropriate data structures for real-world problems. Implements efficient algorithms and understands common patterns.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs custom data structures for performance-critical systems. Expert in algorithm optimization and computational complexity.

skill_library.name: Linux Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Working knowledge of Linux operating systems including command line, file systems, permissions, processes, package management, and basic system administration.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Navigates the file system, runs basic commands, understands permissions and processes.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Comfortable with shell scripting, service management (systemd), networking basics, and package management.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert in Linux internals, kernel concepts, performance tuning, and system administration at scale.

skill_library.name: Database Design & Management
skill_library.description: Design, query, and manage relational and NoSQL databases including schema design, indexing, query optimization, data modeling, and database selection for different use cases.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic SQL queries. Understands relational database concepts (tables, joins, indexes).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs efficient schemas, optimizes queries, and works with both SQL (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis) databases.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects database solutions for high-scale systems. Expert in performance tuning, replication, sharding, and data modeling trade-offs.

skill_library.name: API Design & Development
skill_library.description: Designing and building APIs (REST, GraphQL, gRPC) with proper conventions, versioning, authentication, rate limiting, documentation, and error handling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build and consume basic REST APIs. Understands HTTP methods, status codes, and JSON payloads.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs well-structured APIs with proper versioning, authentication, pagination, and error handling. Experience with API documentation tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects API platforms with gRPC, GraphQL, and REST. Expert in API governance, backward compatibility, and high-performance API design.

skill_library.name: Testing & Quality Practices
skill_library.description: Writing and maintaining automated tests at multiple levels (unit, integration, end-to-end) and implementing quality practices including TDD, code coverage, and testing strategy.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic unit tests. Understands the testing pyramid and the purpose of different test types.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements comprehensive test suites with unit, integration, and e2e tests. Uses mocking, fixtures, and CI-integrated testing.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines organization-wide testing strategies. Expert in test architecture, performance testing, chaos engineering, and test infrastructure.

skill_library.name: Cloud Computing Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Basic understanding of cloud computing concepts including compute, storage, networking, and managed services across major cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure).

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands cloud vs. on-prem trade-offs. Can deploy basic services using a cloud provider's console or CLI.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Works with core cloud services (compute, storage, databases, networking). Understands IAM, VPCs, and basic security.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs cloud architectures with proper service selection, cost optimization, and security hardening.

skill_library.name: Cloud Platforms (AWS/GCP/Azure)
skill_library.description: Deep expertise with one or more major cloud platforms including advanced services, architecture patterns, cost optimization, and multi-cloud strategies.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Uses core services (EC2/GCE, S3/GCS, RDS) and understands cloud networking basics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs solutions using advanced services (Lambda, EKS, BigQuery, Pub/Sub). Implements proper IAM, monitoring, and cost management.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-scale cloud solutions. Expert in multi-account strategies, compliance, disaster recovery, and multi-cloud deployments.

skill_library.name: Performance Optimization
skill_library.description: Profiling, identifying, and resolving performance bottlenecks across systems including CPU, memory, network, and I/O. Covers application-level, database, and infrastructure optimization.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can identify obvious performance issues and apply basic optimization techniques (caching, query optimization).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses profiling tools to identify bottlenecks. Optimizes across application, database, and infrastructure layers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Drives systematic performance engineering across large-scale systems. Expert in latency analysis, resource optimization, and capacity planning.

skill_library.name: Technical Leadership
skill_library.description: Leading technical direction, making architectural decisions, driving engineering best practices, and influencing technical outcomes across teams without necessarily managing people directly.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Leads technical discussions within a team. Makes sound design decisions for features and components.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Drives technical direction for a product area. Sets engineering standards and leads cross-team technical initiatives.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines organization-wide technical vision. Influences architectural decisions across multiple product lines and mentors technical leaders.

skill_library.name: Mentoring & Coaching Engineers
skill_library.description: Developing other engineers through code reviews, pair programming, design reviews, career guidance, and knowledge sharing to elevate team capability and individual growth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides helpful feedback in code reviews. Occasionally pairs with junior engineers on specific tasks.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Actively mentors multiple engineers. Leads design reviews and introduces engineering patterns that improve team practices.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Develops senior engineers into tech leads. Creates mentorship programs and systematically elevates organizational engineering capability.

skill_library.name: Code Review Practices
skill_library.description: Conducting and participating in effective code reviews that improve code quality, share knowledge, and maintain engineering standards across the team.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides basic feedback on code correctness and style. Responds constructively to review feedback.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Reviews code for design, performance, testability, and maintainability. Provides actionable, educational feedback.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines code review standards and processes for the organization. Balances thoroughness with velocity and uses reviews as a mentorship tool.

skill_library.name: CI/CD Pipelines
skill_library.description: Building, maintaining, and optimizing continuous integration and continuous deployment pipelines for automated testing, building, and deploying software.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands CI/CD concepts. Can work within existing pipelines and troubleshoot basic failures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds and maintains CI/CD pipelines with automated testing, linting, and deployment stages. Experience with GitHub Actions, Jenkins, or similar.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects CI/CD infrastructure for organizations. Expert in pipeline optimization, deployment strategies (blue-green, canary), and release management.

skill_library.name: Engineering People Management
skill_library.description: Managing engineering teams including hiring, performance reviews, career development, goal setting, and creating an environment where engineers thrive and deliver their best work.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages a small team (3-5 engineers). Conducts 1:1s, sets basic goals, and handles day-to-day team coordination.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a full team (5-10 engineers). Drives hiring, performance management, career development, and builds team culture.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages managers and large organizations (15+ engineers). Designs org structures, develops leadership pipeline, and drives engineering culture at scale.

skill_library.name: Engineering Hiring & Talent Acquisition
skill_library.description: Recruiting and evaluating engineering talent including defining role requirements, designing interview processes, conducting technical interviews, and building high-performing teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in interviews and provides structured candidate feedback. Helps define role requirements.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs interview processes for engineering roles. Conducts system design and coding interviews. Makes hiring decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds hiring strategies for scaling engineering organizations. Defines interview rubrics, sources talent, and creates employer brand.

skill_library.name: Performance Management
skill_library.description: Setting expectations, conducting performance reviews, providing feedback, coaching for improvement, and managing underperformance in engineering teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Sets clear expectations and provides regular feedback. Conducts basic performance reviews.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs performance frameworks with clear criteria. Handles difficult performance conversations and creates improvement plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds org-wide performance management systems. Develops calibration processes and links performance to career growth frameworks.

skill_library.name: Agile Methodology & Sprint Management
skill_library.description: Applying Agile/Scrum practices including sprint planning, backlog management, retrospectives, velocity tracking, and continuous delivery in engineering teams.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in sprint ceremonies. Understands user stories, estimation, and sprint workflow.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Facilitates sprint planning, retrospectives, and daily standups. Manages backlog and drives sprint delivery.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Adapts Agile practices to organizational needs. Coaches teams on Agile maturity and implements scaled Agile frameworks.

skill_library.name: Cross-Team Collaboration & Coordination
skill_library.description: Working effectively across engineering teams and with product, design, QA, and business stakeholders to drive aligned execution on shared goals and complex initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Collaborates effectively with immediate team and adjacent functions. Communicates status and blockers clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads cross-team initiatives and resolves inter-team dependencies. Aligns multiple stakeholders on technical decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Orchestrates complex, multi-team programs. Drives organizational alignment on strategic technical initiatives.

skill_library.name: Engineering Organization Leadership
skill_library.description: Leading engineering organizations at scale including organizational design, talent strategy, engineering culture, process excellence, and alignment between engineering execution and business goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Leads a single engineering team with clear goals and processes. Builds team culture and ensures delivery.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads multiple teams through managers. Designs org structures, drives process improvements, and aligns engineering with product strategy.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads entire engineering organizations. Defines engineering vision, drives major transformations, and represents engineering at the executive level.

skill_library.name: Engineering Organizational Design
skill_library.description: Designing engineering team structures, reporting lines, and collaboration models that optimize for delivery speed, quality, and engineer satisfaction at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands different team structures (feature teams, platform teams, squads). Can articulate trade-offs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs team structures for a product area. Balances team autonomy with organizational alignment.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs org structures for entire engineering functions. Expert in scaling team models, matrix organizations, and platform/product team dynamics.

skill_library.name: Executive Leadership & Strategy
skill_library.description: Operating at the executive level including setting technology vision, managing budgets, communicating with board/investors, making strategic trade-offs, and driving company-wide initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in executive discussions. Can articulate engineering strategy and trade-offs to senior leadership.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads executive-level initiatives. Manages budgets, drives strategic planning, and influences company direction.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Operates as a full executive leader. Drives company strategy, manages P&L, and represents the company externally.

skill_library.name: Talent Strategy & Workforce Planning
skill_library.description: Strategic planning of engineering workforce including headcount planning, skill gap analysis, succession planning, retention strategies, and building a leadership pipeline.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Identifies hiring needs and skill gaps within a team. Creates basic job descriptions and hiring plans.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops talent strategies for multiple teams. Plans succession, identifies high-potential engineers, and implements retention programs.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs company-wide engineering talent strategy. Builds employer brand, creates leadership development programs, and drives organizational growth.

skill_library.name: AI Strategy & Adoption
skill_library.description: Driving strategic adoption of AI across engineering workflows, product development, and organizational processes. Includes evaluating AI tools, defining AI-first engineering practices, and building AI capabilities into products.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Uses AI tools personally for development productivity. Advocates for AI adoption within the team.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Defines AI-first engineering practices for teams. Evaluates and integrates AI tools into development workflows. Drives AI feature integration into products.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets organization-wide AI strategy. Leads AI transformation initiatives, builds AI capabilities into the platform, and drives measurable impact from AI adoption.

skill_library.name: Stakeholder Management
skill_library.description: Managing relationships and communication with diverse stakeholders including product, sales, customers, and executive leadership to drive aligned decision-making and execution.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Communicates clearly with immediate stakeholders. Manages expectations on timelines and deliverables.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Navigates complex stakeholder dynamics across product, engineering, and business functions. Influences without authority.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages executive-level stakeholder relationships. Drives strategic alignment and represents engineering in company-wide decisions.

skill_library.name: Manual Testing
skill_library.description: Performing manual software testing including functional testing, regression testing, exploratory testing, and UI/UX validation to ensure software quality.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Executes test cases and reports bugs with clear reproduction steps. Performs basic functional and regression testing.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs test strategies and performs exploratory testing. Identifies edge cases and validates complex user flows across platforms.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads test planning for major releases. Expert in risk-based testing, cross-platform testing strategies, and quality assessment.

skill_library.name: Test Automation
skill_library.description: Building and maintaining automated test suites using frameworks like Playwright, Cypress, or Selenium for end-to-end, API, and regression testing.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic automated tests using an existing framework. Can maintain and update existing test suites.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and implements automated test suites with page objects, fixtures, and CI integration. Experienced with Playwright or Cypress.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects test automation frameworks from scratch. Expert in parallel execution, visual regression, and AI-assisted test generation.

skill_library.name: Test Planning & Strategy
skill_library.description: Creating comprehensive test plans, defining test scope, identifying risk areas, and establishing testing strategies that ensure coverage and quality for releases.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic test cases for assigned features. Understands test coverage concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive test plans with risk-based prioritization. Defines test scope and coverage for feature releases.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines org-wide testing strategies. Expert in shift-left testing, test architecture, and quality gate definition.

skill_library.name: Bug Tracking & Defect Management
skill_library.description: Identifying, documenting, tracking, and managing software defects through their lifecycle using bug tracking tools and clear communication practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Reports bugs with clear steps to reproduce, expected vs. actual behavior, and severity classification.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages defect lifecycle including prioritization, tracking trends, and coordinating fixes with developers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Analyzes defect patterns to drive process improvements. Builds defect tracking workflows and quality dashboards.

skill_library.name: API Testing
skill_library.description: Testing APIs for functionality, performance, security, and reliability using tools like Postman, automated test scripts, or API testing frameworks.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Tests API endpoints manually using Postman. Validates responses against expected outputs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Writes automated API test suites. Tests error handling, edge cases, authentication, and performance under load.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive API testing strategies including contract testing, load testing, and security testing.

skill_library.name: QA Methodology & Processes
skill_library.description: Knowledge of QA methodologies including Agile QA, shift-left testing, risk-based testing, release gating, and continuous quality improvement practices.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic QA processes and Agile QA workflow. Follows established testing procedures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements QA best practices within a team. Drives shift-left testing and integrates QA into the development lifecycle.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines QA strategy and methodology for an organization. Leads continuous quality improvement and introduces AI-assisted QA practices.

skill_library.name: Analytical Thinking
skill_library.description: Breaking down complex problems into components, analyzing root causes, evaluating evidence, and making logical, data-informed decisions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can break down simple problems and identify basic cause-effect relationships.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Analyzes complex multi-variable problems. Uses data and evidence to support conclusions and decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Drives strategic analysis of ambiguous, high-stakes problems. Builds analytical frameworks used by teams and organizations.

skill_library.name: Cloud Platforms for DevOps
skill_library.description: Deep operational expertise with cloud platforms focused on infrastructure management, networking, IAM, cost optimization, and service deployment at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages basic cloud resources. Understands IAM, VPCs, and core compute/storage services.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and manages cloud infrastructure including networking, multi-account strategies, and cost optimization.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-scale cloud environments. Expert in multi-cloud, compliance, disaster recovery, and infrastructure security.

skill_library.name: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
skill_library.description: Defining and managing infrastructure through code using tools like Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation, or AWS CDK for reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes basic Terraform/CloudFormation templates. Understands state management and resource definitions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds modular, reusable IaC with proper state management, environments, and CI/CD integration.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects IaC frameworks for organizations. Expert in multi-account provisioning, drift detection, and IaC governance.

skill_library.name: Containerization & Orchestration
skill_library.description: Working with container technologies (Docker) and orchestration platforms (Kubernetes) for packaging, deploying, and managing applications at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates Dockerfiles and runs containers. Understands basic Kubernetes concepts (pods, services, deployments).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages Kubernetes clusters with Helm charts, ConfigMaps, secrets, and networking. Implements deployment strategies.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects Kubernetes-based platforms. Expert in custom operators, multi-cluster management, service mesh, and Kubernetes security.

skill_library.name: Linux System Administration
skill_library.description: Advanced Linux system management including systemd, networking, storage, security hardening, performance tuning, and troubleshooting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages Linux services, users, and packages. Basic understanding of systemd and networking.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Administers production Linux systems. Experienced with networking configuration, storage management, and security hardening.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert in Linux internals, kernel tuning, performance analysis, and managing Linux at enterprise scale.

skill_library.name: Scripting & Automation
skill_library.description: Writing scripts in Python, Bash, or similar languages to automate operational tasks, infrastructure management, deployments, and repetitive workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Writes simple Bash/Python scripts for basic automation tasks.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds robust automation scripts with error handling, logging, and integration with APIs and cloud services.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive automation frameworks. Expert in building self-service tooling and intelligent automation systems.

skill_library.name: Monitoring & Observability
skill_library.description: Implementing monitoring, logging, alerting, and observability solutions to ensure system health, detect issues, and enable fast incident resolution.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Sets up basic monitoring dashboards and alerts. Understands metrics, logs, and traces.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs observability stacks with proper metric collection, log aggregation, distributed tracing, and alert tuning.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise observability platforms. Expert in SLI/SLO definition, AI-powered anomaly detection, and observability-driven development.

skill_library.name: Networking Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Understanding of network concepts including TCP/IP, DNS, load balancing, VPCs, firewalls, VPNs, and network security relevant to cloud and infrastructure engineering.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, and basic network troubleshooting.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Configures VPCs, subnets, load balancers, and security groups. Understands network architectures for cloud environments.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs complex network architectures including multi-region, hybrid cloud, and secure tunnel configurations.

skill_library.name: Security Best Practices
skill_library.description: Implementing security practices across infrastructure and application layers including IAM, encryption, secrets management, vulnerability management, and compliance.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Follows security best practices for IAM, secrets, and basic encryption. Understands OWASP top 10.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements security controls across CI/CD, infrastructure, and applications. Experienced with secrets management and compliance frameworks.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs security architectures for organizations. Expert in DevSecOps, threat modeling, and security compliance at scale.

skill_library.name: Production Systems Operations
skill_library.description: Operating and maintaining production systems including deployment management, incident response, capacity planning, and ensuring system reliability and availability.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in on-call. Can follow runbooks and escalate issues appropriately.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages production deployments and incident response. Implements deployment strategies (blue-green, canary) and capacity planning.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs production operations practices for organizations. Expert in chaos engineering, SRE principles, and production reliability at scale.

skill_library.name: Incident Management & Response
skill_library.description: Managing production incidents from detection through resolution, including on-call practices, escalation procedures, communication, root cause analysis, and post-mortem processes.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Responds to incidents following established procedures. Documents incidents and participates in post-mortems.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads incident response. Conducts root cause analysis, writes post-mortems with actionable follow-ups, and improves incident processes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs incident management frameworks for organizations. Builds AI-powered incident detection and automated mitigation systems.

skill_library.name: Communication Skills
skill_library.description: Clear written and verbal communication for technical discussions, documentation, presentations, cross-team collaboration, and stakeholder updates.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Communicates clearly in team settings. Writes understandable documentation and bug reports.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Presents technical concepts to diverse audiences. Writes effective technical proposals, design docs, and status reports.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Communicates at the executive level. Influences organizational decisions through clear articulation of technical strategy and trade-offs.

skill_library.name: LLM API Integration
skill_library.description: Ability to integrate and work effectively with commercial LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, AWS Bedrock, Google Gemini) including authentication, rate limiting, streaming, structured outputs, function calling, and error handling. Core skill for any production AI engineering work.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can make basic API calls to LLM providers, handle simple prompts, parse responses. Familiar with one or two major providers.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Proficient with multiple LLM providers, implements streaming, structured outputs, function/tool calling, and manages rate limits and error handling in production. Understands cost and latency tradeoffs.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs multi-provider LLM orchestration layers with fallback, routing, and cost optimization. Architects systems abstracted over LLM providers with clean evaluation and swap-out. Deep understanding of each provider's capabilities and limits.

skill_library.name: Prompt Engineering
skill_library.description: Craft of designing, optimizing, and maintaining prompts for LLMs to produce reliable, accurate, and useful outputs. Includes few-shot learning, chain-of-thought reasoning, structured outputs, role prompting, and systematic iteration based on evaluation.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can write basic prompts that produce reasonable outputs. Familiar with simple techniques like role assignment and examples.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Applies advanced techniques (few-shot, chain-of-thought, structured outputs, self-consistency). Iterates systematically based on evaluation. Builds reusable prompt templates.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs complex prompt systems with evaluation frameworks. Identifies and mitigates subtle failure modes. Maintains production prompt libraries with versioning. Contributes to team standards and mentors others.

skill_library.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Systems
skill_library.description: Design and implementation of retrieval-augmented generation pipelines: document processing, chunking strategies, embedding generation, vector search, reranking, and context assembly for LLM consumption. Critical for most production AI applications involving knowledge bases or custom data.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build basic RAG pipeline with off-the-shelf tools. Understands embedding concepts and vector search basics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Implements production RAG with thoughtful chunking, hybrid search, reranking, and evaluation. Optimizes retrieval quality and handles edge cases (multi-document, structured data, long contexts).
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex RAG systems at scale: multi-stage retrieval, graph-RAG, agentic retrieval, domain-specific embeddings. Designs evaluation harnesses for retrieval quality. Solves hard problems in hallucination mitigation through retrieval design.

skill_library.name: AI Agent & Agentic Workflow Development
skill_library.description: Building autonomous or semi-autonomous AI agents that use tools, maintain state, make decisions, and complete multi-step tasks. Includes agent frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, Claude/OpenAI Agents SDK), tool design, orchestration patterns, and production reliability.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build simple single-agent systems with basic tool use. Familiar with one agent framework.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds multi-step agents with complex tool interactions, state management, and error recovery. Uses multiple frameworks and understands tradeoffs between them.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects multi-agent systems with orchestration layers, specialized agents, and production reliability. Designs custom agent frameworks or heavily extends existing ones. Solves hard problems in agent reliability, cost, and performance.

skill_library.name: Vector Databases & Embedding Systems
skill_library.description: Working with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Chroma, pgvector, FAISS) for similarity search, semantic retrieval, and embedding storage at scale. Includes index design, query optimization, hybrid search, and operational management.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use vector databases for basic similarity search. Understands embedding concepts and basic index types.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Proficient with multiple vector databases, designs schemas for production use, implements hybrid search, manages indexes at scale.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep expertise across vector DB architectures, chooses appropriate solutions for different use cases, optimizes for cost/latency/accuracy at scale. Implements custom retrieval strategies.

skill_library.name: LLM Evaluation & Testing
skill_library.description: Designing and implementing evaluation frameworks for LLM-based systems, including offline benchmarks, LLM-as-judge patterns, human evaluation workflows, regression testing, and production monitoring for accuracy, quality, and safety.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can run basic evaluations using standard benchmarks or manual review. Understands evaluation concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds systematic evaluation pipelines with automated metrics, LLM-as-judge, and regression testing. Integrates evaluation into development workflow.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive evaluation architectures across multiple dimensions (accuracy, safety, cost, latency). Creates custom evaluation methodologies for novel problems. Establishes evaluation as core engineering discipline.

skill_library.name: Machine Learning Fundamentals
skill_library.description: Strong grounding in classical ML methods: supervised and unsupervised learning, feature engineering, model selection, evaluation metrics, cross-validation, regularization, and common algorithms (trees, ensembles, linear models, clustering). Foundation for applied AI work beyond LLMs.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands core ML concepts and can build basic models using scikit-learn or similar. Knows common algorithms and evaluation metrics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Applies ML effectively to production problems, selects appropriate algorithms, handles real-world data issues, designs feature engineering strategies, evaluates rigorously.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep expertise across ML methodologies, contributes novel approaches, handles complex scenarios (imbalanced data, causal inference, interpretability). Mentors others on ML craft.

skill_library.name: Deep Learning
skill_library.description: Designing, training, and deploying neural networks: architectures (CNNs, RNNs, Transformers), frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow), training optimization, transfer learning, and fine-tuning. Includes understanding of modern architectures and training dynamics.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can train simple neural networks using high-level APIs. Understands basic architectures and training concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production DL models, understands transformer architectures deeply, applies transfer learning, handles training optimization and debugging.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs novel architectures, optimizes training at scale, contributes to research-level problems, mentors on DL methodology and implementation.

skill_library.name: Model Training & Fine-tuning
skill_library.description: Training ML models and fine-tuning pre-trained models (including LLMs) for specific tasks or domains. Includes dataset preparation, training pipeline design, hyperparameter tuning, LoRA/PEFT methods, evaluation, and iterative refinement.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can run pre-built training scripts, basic fine-tuning of small models using standard tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs fine-tuning pipelines, applies parameter-efficient methods (LoRA, adapters), manages training datasets, evaluates systematically, iterates based on results.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads complex fine-tuning projects for LLMs or large models, designs training infrastructure, solves hard problems in training stability, data quality, and evaluation. Contributes to methodology.

skill_library.name: Computer Vision
skill_library.description: Building systems that extract information from images, video, and visual sensor data: detection, segmentation, tracking, classification, OCR, and scene understanding. Includes classical CV algorithms and modern deep learning approaches.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use pre-trained CV models via standard libraries (OpenCV, Hugging Face). Understands basic concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production CV systems, trains custom models, applies appropriate architectures for different problems (YOLO, ViT, SAM), handles real-world data challenges.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex CV pipelines, works with multi-sensor systems, optimizes for edge deployment, solves hard problems in degraded/noisy conditions. Deep expertise in both classical and DL approaches.

skill_library.name: Natural Language Processing
skill_library.description: Working with text data and language models for tasks like classification, entity extraction, sentiment analysis, summarization, translation, and generation. Includes both classical NLP and modern transformer-based approaches.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can apply pre-trained NLP models for common tasks using standard libraries. Understands core concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production NLP systems, handles domain-specific language problems, combines classical and modern techniques, evaluates systematically.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep expertise across NLP methodologies, handles complex multilingual or specialized domains, contributes to novel approaches, mentors on NLP craft.

skill_library.name: MLOps Pipeline Design
skill_library.description: Building end-to-end ML pipelines covering the full model lifecycle: data ingestion, training orchestration, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, and retraining. Includes reproducibility, versioning, and automation across the lifecycle.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build simple training and deployment workflows using standard tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs production MLOps pipelines with reproducibility, experiment tracking, and automated deployment. Uses Airflow, Prefect, or similar orchestration tools effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects comprehensive MLOps platforms, solves hard problems in reproducibility at scale, automated retraining, and lifecycle management across many models and teams.

skill_library.name: Model Deployment & Serving
skill_library.description: Deploying trained models to production: batch inference pipelines, real-time serving APIs, edge deployment, rollback strategies, A/B testing, and canary deployments. Includes containerization and orchestration for model workloads.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can deploy simple models via REST APIs or batch jobs using standard tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs production deployment strategies, handles real-time serving with appropriate latency and scaling, implements rollback and monitoring.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects multi-environment deployment systems (cloud, on-prem, edge), optimizes for cost and performance at scale, designs sophisticated rollout strategies, solves hard problems in model serving reliability.

skill_library.name: Model Monitoring & Drift Detection
skill_library.description: Monitoring ML models in production for performance, data drift, and quality degradation. Includes designing metrics, alerting, drift detection methodologies, and continuous evaluation pipelines to ensure models remain accurate as real-world conditions change.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands monitoring concepts, can set up basic metrics tracking for deployed models.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive monitoring systems with drift detection, quality metrics, and automated alerting. Integrates with retraining pipelines.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds sophisticated observability platforms for ML at scale, handles complex drift scenarios (concept drift, data drift, label drift), designs continuous evaluation architectures.

skill_library.name: Distributed Training & GPU Optimization
skill_library.description: Training ML models at scale using distributed computing: multi-GPU training, distributed data parallelism, model parallelism, Ray/Horovod/DDP. Includes GPU workload optimization for throughput, memory, and cost efficiency.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands distributed training concepts, can run pre-configured distributed jobs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and optimizes distributed training pipelines, handles GPU memory issues, applies appropriate parallelism strategies.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects large-scale training systems, optimizes training efficiency across complex workloads, handles frontier model training challenges, contributes to infrastructure design.

skill_library.name: Edge AI & Embedded Deployment
skill_library.description: Deploying AI models on edge devices with constrained resources: Jetson platforms, mobile devices, embedded systems. Includes model optimization (quantization, pruning, distillation), inference engines (TensorRT, ONNX), and real-time performance tuning.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands edge deployment concepts, can run models on simple edge platforms.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Optimizes models for edge deployment using quantization and specialized runtimes. Handles real-time performance requirements on Jetson or similar platforms.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex edge AI systems, solves hard problems in latency/memory/power constraints, designs multi-sensor real-time pipelines on edge hardware.

skill_library.name: CUDA & GPU Programming
skill_library.description: Low-level GPU programming with CUDA or similar frameworks for performance-critical components of AI systems. Includes kernel development, memory management, and integration with deep learning frameworks.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands GPU computing concepts, can use high-level GPU APIs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Writes custom CUDA kernels for specific performance needs, optimizes GPU memory and throughput for ML workloads.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep expertise in GPU architecture, writes highly optimized custom kernels, solves hardest performance problems in ML infrastructure, contributes to systems design.

skill_library.name: Multi-Sensor Fusion & Integration
skill_library.description: Building AI systems that process and integrate data from multiple sensor modalities: RGB, thermal, radar, lidar, depth, audio. Includes sensor calibration, temporal synchronization, cross-modal ground truth, and fusion architectures.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands multi-sensor concepts, has worked with basic sensor integration.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds production multi-sensor pipelines, handles calibration and synchronization, applies fusion techniques for specific problems.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex multi-sensor AI systems, handles challenging real-world conditions across modalities, solves hard problems in cross-sensor learning and ground truth transfer.

skill_library.name: Applied AI Research Methodology
skill_library.description: Ability to frame open-ended AI problems as research questions, design rigorous experiments, read and apply academic literature, and translate research ideas into production systems. Bridges academic research and applied engineering.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can read and understand AI papers, run experiments based on established methodologies.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Frames novel research questions from business problems, designs rigorous experiments, applies cutting-edge methods from literature to production problems.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Contributes novel methodologies, publishes or presents research, leads open-ended research programs, bridges research to production reliably for complex problems.

skill_library.name: AI Safety & Responsible AI
skill_library.description: Practices and techniques for building safe, reliable, and trustworthy AI systems: hallucination mitigation, bias detection and correction, guardrails, content safety, privacy preservation, and alignment with intended use cases.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Aware of AI safety concepts, can apply basic safety controls (content filters, prompt safety).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs safety systems into production AI: comprehensive guardrails, evaluation for harmful outputs, hallucination mitigation, bias testing.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads responsible AI practices across teams, contributes to safety methodologies, handles complex scenarios involving regulation, high-stakes decisions, or vulnerable users.

skill_library.name: Conversational AI & Dialogue Design
skill_library.description: Designing multi-turn conversational experiences for text and voice agents: dialogue flows, context management, memory handling, persona consistency, tone and style, multilingual support, and conversation evaluation.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design simple conversational flows and prompts for chatbots.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs sophisticated multi-turn dialogues with context awareness, persona consistency, and multilingual handling. Iterates based on real conversation data.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads conversational design systems at scale, handles complex scenarios (voice, regulated industries, nuanced personas), establishes team standards and mentors others.

skill_library.name: AI Product Thinking
skill_library.description: Ability to identify high-value AI use cases, evaluate feasibility (technical, data, organizational), and design AI features that deliver real user value. Combines AI technical knowledge with product sense and business judgment.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can identify obvious AI use cases, understands basic feasibility concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Systematically evaluates AI opportunities across impact, feasibility, and data readiness. Designs AI features with clear user value and measurable outcomes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads AI product strategy, makes complex build-vs-buy decisions, identifies non-obvious high-leverage AI applications, shapes product roadmap.

skill_library.name: AI Transformation & Change Management
skill_library.description: Leading organizational adoption of AI: identifying internal use cases, building adoption programs, training teams, managing change resistance, and measuring transformation outcomes. Distinct from building AI products for external customers.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands AI transformation concepts, can support adoption initiatives.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads AI tool rollouts end-to-end: use case identification, business case, rollout, training, measurement. Handles change management effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise AI transformation programs, leads across complex organizations, establishes governance frameworks, delivers measurable business impact at scale.

skill_library.name: AI Governance & Compliance
skill_library.description: Establishing frameworks for responsible AI use in organizations: AI usage policies, model risk management, privacy and data protection in AI systems, audit trails, regulatory compliance (especially in regulated industries like finance, healthcare).

proficiency_levels.beginner: Familiar with AI governance concepts and major frameworks.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Establishes AI governance practices within a team or business unit: policies, review processes, risk assessment.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads enterprise AI governance programs, handles complex regulatory environments, represents the organization on AI compliance externally.

skill_library.name: AI Team Leadership
skill_library.description: Leading teams of AI engineers, researchers, and MLOps specialists. Includes hiring for AI roles, setting technical direction, balancing research and delivery, managing career development, and building AI engineering culture.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Has led small technical initiatives or mentored individual engineers.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages AI team of 3-10 people, sets technical direction, hires effectively, balances research and production work.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads large AI organizations (10+ engineers), develops AI leaders, establishes hiring and evaluation standards, drives strategic AI direction across a company.

skill_library.name: AI Strategy & Roadmap Development
skill_library.description: Developing AI strategy for products and organizations: identifying where AI creates competitive advantage, building multi-year AI roadmaps, making build-vs-buy-vs-partner decisions, and aligning AI investment with business goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Contributes to AI strategy discussions, understands strategic concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Owns AI strategy for a product or business unit, makes significant architectural and investment decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines company-wide AI strategy, represents AI vision externally, makes executive-level decisions on AI as competitive differentiator.

skill_library.name: No-Code / Low-Code AI Automation
skill_library.description: Building AI-powered workflows using no-code and low-code platforms (Zapier, Make, n8n, Workato) combined with LLM APIs and custom logic. Enables rapid automation of business processes without deep engineering.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build simple automations in one platform using AI tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs production-grade automation workflows across multiple platforms, integrates with enterprise systems, handles error cases and monitoring.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex automation ecosystems, builds custom integrations and agents, handles scale and reliability for business-critical workflows.

skill_library.name: Customer-Facing AI Solution Delivery
skill_library.description: Ability to work directly with customers to understand their processes and design AI-powered solutions. Combines technical AI knowledge, consultative customer engagement, and delivery execution. Common in AI platform startups and consulting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Has customer-facing experience in technical context, can support AI implementations with guidance.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Owns customer AI delivery end-to-end: discovery, design, implementation, adoption. Navigates complex stakeholder environments.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads delivery for strategic customers, manages complex transformations, sets delivery standards, designs reusable assets and processes.

skill_library.name: ML Systems Thinking
skill_library.description: Holistic understanding of how ML/AI systems work end-to-end: data flow, training dynamics, deployment tradeoffs, feedback loops, failure modes, and how research choices affect production behavior. Essential for senior AI roles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands individual components of ML systems in isolation.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Connects components into coherent mental model, anticipates system-level issues, designs systems that work end-to-end.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep systems intuition across complex AI architectures, identifies non-obvious failure modes, architects systems that are robust and maintainable at scale.

skill_library.name: ML Data Pipeline Engineering
skill_library.description: Building and maintaining data pipelines specifically for ML workloads: ingestion of structured and unstructured data, feature engineering, labeling workflows, dataset versioning, and serving data to training and inference systems.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can build basic data pipelines using standard tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs production ML data pipelines with versioning, quality controls, and appropriate orchestration. Handles large datasets and complex transformations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects comprehensive data platforms for ML at scale, solves hard problems in data quality, labeling workflows, and dataset management.

skill_library.name: AI Experiment Tracking & Reproducibility
skill_library.description: Using tools like MLflow, Weights & Biases, or DVC to track experiments, manage model versions, ensure reproducibility, and collaborate across AI teams. Essential for rigorous AI development practice.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Uses experiment tracking tools for personal work.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Establishes experiment tracking practices across a team, ensures reproducibility, uses tracking data to drive decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs experiment tracking infrastructure at scale, sets organizational standards, builds custom tooling where needed.

skill_library.name: AI Cost & Performance Optimization
skill_library.description: Managing and reducing costs of AI systems at scale: model selection tradeoffs, caching strategies, prompt optimization, batching, smaller model routing, and inference efficiency. Includes latency optimization alongside cost.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Aware of AI cost drivers, applies basic optimization.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Systematically optimizes AI system costs across model choice, caching, prompt design, and infrastructure. Measures impact rigorously.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects AI systems with cost as first-class design constraint at scale. Solves hard problems in latency/cost/quality tradeoffs.

skill_library.name: AI Talent Acquisition & Hiring
skill_library.description: Hiring for AI roles: defining requirements for specialized positions, evaluating technical depth in AI interviews, assessing research vs. engineering balance, and building diverse AI teams. Critical for AI leadership roles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in AI hiring as interviewer or hiring manager for individual roles.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Owns hiring for AI team: defines roles, designs interview processes, makes final decisions, builds pipelines.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads AI talent acquisition at scale, designs hiring systems for multiple AI functions, represents company externally to AI talent.

skill_library.name: LLM Fundamentals & Understanding
skill_library.description: Core conceptual understanding of how large language models work: transformer architecture, training objectives, tokenization, context windows, sampling strategies, and capabilities/limitations. Foundation for any AI engineering work with LLMs.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands LLMs as black-box tools, basic concepts like context and tokens.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Solid grounding in LLM mechanics, understands why models behave as they do, applies that understanding to practical problems.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Deep understanding of LLM architecture and training, follows frontier research, can reason about novel capabilities and failure modes.

skill_library.name: Change Management & Organizational Adoption
skill_library.description: Leading organizational change initiatives: stakeholder alignment, communication planning, resistance management, training and enablement, adoption measurement, and sustaining new ways of working. Applicable across technology transformations, process changes, and organizational restructuring.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands change management concepts, can support adoption initiatives and create basic enablement materials.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads change programs end-to-end: stakeholder mapping, communication plans, training rollouts, resistance management, and adoption tracking. Handles multiple concurrent changes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Drives enterprise-scale transformation programs across complex organizations. Designs change frameworks, manages executive stakeholders, and delivers measurable business outcomes from organizational transitions.

skill_library.name: Figma Mastery
skill_library.description: Expert-level proficiency in Figma including components, variants, auto-layout, design tokens, prototyping, branching, and collaborative workflows. The industry-standard design tool for product and brand design.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic frames, shapes, and text. Understands layers and simple prototyping. Can navigate and comment on shared files.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds reusable components with variants and auto-layout. Creates interactive prototypes with transitions. Uses styles and shared libraries effectively. Comfortable with developer handoff workflows.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects complex component systems with design tokens. Manages branching workflows and multi-file libraries. Builds advanced prototypes with variables and conditional logic. Optimizes file performance for large-scale projects.

skill_library.name: UI & Visual Design
skill_library.description: Craft-level proficiency in visual design fundamentals — typography, color theory, layout, spacing, hierarchy, composition, and pixel-perfect execution. The ability to create polished, aesthetically refined interfaces that are both beautiful and functional.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic typography, color, and layout principles. Can create clean, functional UI following established guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Strong command of visual hierarchy, spacing systems, and typographic scale. Creates polished, consistent interfaces with attention to detail. Develops cohesive visual languages for products.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Sets visual direction for products and brands. Defines typographic systems, color palettes, and spacing frameworks. Creates pixel-perfect designs that balance aesthetics with usability at scale.

skill_library.name: UX Design Process
