skill_library.description: End-to-end user experience design methodology — from discovery and research through ideation, wireframing, prototyping, testing, and iteration. The structured approach to solving user problems and designing intuitive experiences.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands the design thinking framework. Can create basic wireframes and user flows. Participates in usability testing sessions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads end-to-end design processes independently. Conducts user research, creates journey maps, builds prototypes, and iterates based on feedback. Balances user needs with business requirements.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines and evolves design processes for teams. Integrates research, data, and business strategy into design decisions. Mentors others on UX methodology. Drives organizational adoption of user-centered design.

skill_library.name: Interaction Design
skill_library.description: Designing how users interact with digital products — micro-interactions, transitions, animations, gestures, state changes, and feedback patterns. Creating responsive, intuitive behaviors that make interfaces feel natural and delightful.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic interaction patterns (hover, click, scroll). Can specify simple transitions and state changes in designs.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs thoughtful micro-interactions and transition systems. Creates interactive prototypes that communicate complex behaviors. Understands platform-specific interaction conventions (web vs. mobile).
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines interaction design systems and motion principles for products. Creates novel interaction patterns for complex workflows. Balances delight with performance and accessibility.

skill_library.name: Information Architecture
skill_library.description: Structuring and organizing content, features, and navigation in digital products so users can find what they need and complete tasks efficiently. Includes site mapping, content hierarchy, taxonomy design, and navigation patterns.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic sitemaps and navigation structures. Understands content grouping and labeling principles.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs scalable information architectures for complex products. Conducts card sorting and tree testing. Creates effective navigation systems and content hierarchies.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects information systems for multi-product suites and platforms. Defines taxonomies and metadata structures. Optimizes IA for findability, scalability, and cross-product consistency.

skill_library.name: User Research
skill_library.description: Planning, conducting, and synthesizing user research to understand behaviors, needs, and motivations. Includes qualitative methods (interviews, usability testing, contextual inquiry) and quantitative methods (surveys, analytics, A/B testing).

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can conduct basic usability tests and user interviews with a guide. Understands the difference between qualitative and quantitative methods.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Plans and executes research studies independently. Synthesizes findings into actionable insights, personas, and journey maps. Selects appropriate methods for different research questions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds research programs and repositories. Designs complex mixed-methods studies. Influences product strategy through research insights. Mentors teams on research methodology and integration.

skill_library.name: Prototyping
skill_library.description: Creating interactive representations of designs at varying fidelity levels — from paper sketches and clickable wireframes to high-fidelity interactive prototypes. Used to test concepts, communicate design intent, and validate ideas before development.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic clickable prototypes with simple navigation and page transitions in Figma or similar tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex interactive prototypes with conditional logic, variables, and realistic data. Uses prototypes to test hypotheses and communicate nuanced interactions to developers.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates production-quality prototypes that closely simulate the final experience. Uses code-based prototyping when needed. Leverages AI tools to accelerate prototype creation.

skill_library.name: Design System Management
skill_library.description: Building, governing, and evolving design systems — the shared libraries of components, patterns, tokens, and guidelines that ensure consistency and efficiency across products. Includes component architecture, token management, documentation, and adoption strategies.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands what design systems are and can use existing component libraries effectively. Follows established patterns and guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Contributes to design system development. Creates and maintains components with proper variants and documentation. Ensures designs adhere to the system.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects and governs design systems at scale. Defines token structures, component APIs, and contribution workflows. Drives adoption across teams. Collaborates with engineering on component implementation and design-to-code fidelity.

skill_library.name: Data Visualization Design
skill_library.description: Designing clear, effective visual representations of data — dashboards, charts, graphs, tables, and data-heavy interfaces. Translating complex datasets into understandable, actionable visual information.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic charts, tables, and simple data displays. Understands fundamental data visualization principles.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive dashboards and data-intensive interfaces. Selects appropriate chart types for different data stories. Balances information density with readability.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates innovative data visualization systems for complex, multi-dimensional datasets. Defines data display patterns and frameworks for enterprise products. Designs for real-time data and large-scale analytics platforms.

skill_library.name: Mobile Design
skill_library.description: Designing user experiences specifically for mobile platforms (iOS and Android). Includes understanding platform-specific conventions, touch interaction patterns, responsive design, and the unique constraints and opportunities of mobile interfaces.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic mobile UI patterns and platform guidelines (iOS HIG, Material Design). Can adapt web designs for mobile screens.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs native mobile experiences with platform-appropriate patterns. Understands touch targets, gestures, and mobile-specific workflows. Creates responsive designs that work across device sizes.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines mobile design strategies and platform-specific design systems. Designs for complex mobile workflows including offline states, notifications, and device capabilities. Expert in both iOS and Android design conventions.

skill_library.name: Accessibility & Inclusive Design
skill_library.description: Designing products that are usable by people of all abilities. Includes understanding WCAG guidelines, color contrast requirements, screen reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, and inclusive design principles.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic accessibility principles — color contrast, text size, alt text. Aware of WCAG guidelines.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs with accessibility as a core consideration. Ensures proper contrast ratios, keyboard navigation, focus states, and screen reader compatibility. Tests designs against WCAG AA standards.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Champions accessibility across the organization. Defines accessibility standards and testing processes. Designs for diverse abilities including cognitive, motor, and sensory disabilities. Ensures WCAG AAA compliance where appropriate.

skill_library.name: Wireframing & Sketching
skill_library.description: Creating low-fidelity representations of interfaces to explore layout, content hierarchy, and user flows before investing in visual design. Includes hand sketching, digital wireframing, and rapid ideation techniques.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can create basic wireframes showing layout and content placement. Uses wireframes to communicate simple page structures.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Rapidly produces wireframes to explore multiple solutions. Uses wireframes effectively in stakeholder discussions. Creates annotated wireframes that communicate interaction behavior.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Uses wireframing as a strategic tool for rapid exploration and stakeholder alignment. Facilitates collaborative sketching sessions. Knows when to wireframe vs. jump to higher fidelity.

skill_library.name: Usability Testing
skill_library.description: Planning, conducting, and analyzing usability tests to evaluate how well users can complete tasks with a product. Includes moderated and unmoderated testing, task design, participant recruitment, and insight synthesis.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can facilitate basic usability test sessions using a script. Records observations and identifies obvious usability issues.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Plans and conducts usability studies independently. Designs effective test tasks and scenarios. Synthesizes findings into prioritized recommendations. Uses remote and in-person testing tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs rigorous usability testing programs. Combines qualitative observations with quantitative metrics (task success rate, time on task, SUS scores). Integrates usability testing into continuous product development cycles.

skill_library.name: Design Leadership & Team Management
skill_library.description: Leading, mentoring, and managing design teams. Includes setting design vision, building team culture, conducting design critiques, managing career development, recruiting design talent, and elevating design as a strategic function within the organization.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Provides constructive feedback to peers. Participates actively in design critiques. Mentors junior designers informally.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a small design team. Conducts regular critiques and one-on-ones. Hires and onboards designers. Establishes team processes and quality standards.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads a design organization with multiple leads and teams. Shapes design vision and strategy at the executive level. Builds design culture and talent pipelines. Elevates design as a strategic business function.

skill_library.name: Design Critique & Feedback
skill_library.description: Giving and receiving constructive design feedback effectively. Includes facilitating structured critique sessions, articulating design rationale, evaluating designs against user needs and business goals, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can articulate basic design opinions with reasoning. Receives feedback openly and iterates accordingly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Gives clear, actionable feedback grounded in design principles and user needs. Facilitates productive critique sessions. Separates personal preference from objective evaluation.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads organizational critique culture. Calibrates quality standards across teams. Provides feedback that elevates both the work and the designer. Balances craft, user needs, and business constraints in evaluations.

skill_library.name: Brand Identity & Visual Language
skill_library.description: Creating and maintaining cohesive brand identities — logos, color palettes, typography systems, imagery styles, and visual guidelines that define how a company presents itself. Ensuring brand consistency across all touchpoints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can apply existing brand guidelines consistently. Understands basic brand elements (logo usage, color palette, typography).
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops brand visual systems including style guides, asset libraries, and usage documentation. Evolves brand identity while maintaining consistency.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates brand identities from scratch. Defines comprehensive visual languages that scale across products, marketing, and communications. Leads brand evolution and repositioning efforts.

skill_library.name: Marketing Campaign Design
skill_library.description: Designing visual assets for marketing campaigns across digital and physical channels — social media, email, paid ads, landing pages, events, and print. Balancing brand consistency with channel-specific optimization and conversion goals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic marketing assets following brand guidelines. Understands common formats for social media, email, and web.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs comprehensive campaign assets across multiple channels. Adapts creative for different platforms and formats. Understands conversion principles and A/B testing for creative optimization.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads creative direction for large-scale marketing campaigns. Develops campaign visual systems that scale. Integrates performance data into creative decisions. Manages creative output across internal and external teams.

skill_library.name: Presentation & Deck Design
skill_library.description: Creating compelling, visually polished presentations for sales, executive communication, investor relations, and conferences. Includes slide design, data visualization for presentations, visual storytelling, and template system creation.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates clean, readable slides following a template. Understands basic slide layout and content hierarchy.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs compelling presentation decks with strong visual storytelling. Creates reusable templates and slide libraries. Visualizes data effectively for presentation contexts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs high-stakes executive and investor presentations. Creates scalable presentation systems for sales enablement. Translates complex narratives into clear, persuasive visual stories.

skill_library.name: Web Design & Landing Pages
skill_library.description: Designing websites and landing pages that balance brand expression, user experience, and conversion optimization. Includes responsive design, web-specific interaction patterns, SEO-friendly layouts, and understanding of web development constraints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic web pages with proper layout, typography, and responsive considerations. Understands fundamental web conventions.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs conversion-optimized landing pages and multi-page websites. Understands responsive breakpoints, web performance considerations, and SEO-friendly design practices. Works with web builders (Webflow, Elementor).
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads web design strategy and information architecture for complex websites. Optimizes for conversion through data-driven design decisions. Defines web design systems that scale across multiple properties.

skill_library.name: Motion Design & Animation
skill_library.description: Creating animations, motion graphics, and video content that bring brands and interfaces to life. Includes UI animation principles, marketing video production, storyboarding, and motion-based storytelling.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic animation principles (timing, easing). Can create simple UI animations and transitions. Familiar with After Effects or similar tools.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Creates polished motion graphics for marketing and product contexts. Develops storyboards and animated sequences. Produces social media video content and product demos.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Defines motion design language and animation systems for brands. Directs complex animation projects. Creates high-quality motion content across marketing, product, and brand channels.

skill_library.name: AI-Assisted Design Workflows
skill_library.description: Leveraging AI tools to accelerate and enhance the design process — including generative AI for image creation, AI-assisted prototyping, design-to-code tools, research synthesis, and workflow automation. Understanding how to integrate AI into design practice effectively.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Aware of AI design tools and their capabilities. Has experimented with generative image tools (Midjourney, DALL·E) or AI design assistants.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Actively uses AI tools in daily workflow — generative AI for ideation, AI-assisted design-to-code (Figma Make, Cursor), LLMs for research synthesis and content generation. Understands strengths and limitations of AI tools.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Establishes AI-forward design workflows for teams. Builds AI-augmented design pipelines that significantly accelerate output. Evaluates and adopts new AI tools strategically. Helps define how AI changes the design process organizationally.

skill_library.name: Generative AI for Creative Production
skill_library.description: Using generative AI tools (Midjourney, DALL·E, Adobe Firefly, Stable Diffusion) to create, enhance, and iterate on visual content for brand, marketing, and product design. Includes prompt engineering for visual output, style control, and integrating AI-generated assets into professional workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can generate basic images using text prompts. Understands the capabilities and limitations of major generative AI platforms.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Uses generative AI tools effectively for ideation, asset creation, and visual exploration. Controls style, composition, and quality through advanced prompting. Integrates AI-generated assets into professional design workflows.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds creative production pipelines using generative AI. Combines multiple AI tools for complex visual outputs. Establishes best practices for AI-assisted creative production at team scale. Understands ethical and legal considerations of AI-generated content.

skill_library.name: Adobe Creative Suite
skill_library.description: Proficiency in Adobe's design applications — Photoshop for image editing, Illustrator for vector graphics, InDesign for print layouts, After Effects for animation, and Premiere Pro for video editing.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can perform basic tasks in Photoshop and Illustrator — image editing, simple vector creation, and basic layouts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Proficient across multiple Adobe applications. Creates professional-quality graphics, illustrations, photo compositions, and print layouts. Comfortable with After Effects for basic motion design.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert-level proficiency across the full Adobe suite. Creates complex compositions, illustrations, and motion graphics. Develops efficient production workflows using Adobe tools. Mentors others on advanced techniques.

skill_library.name: Design Communication & Stakeholder Management
skill_library.description: Articulating design rationale, presenting design work to stakeholders, securing alignment on design direction, and managing expectations across product, engineering, and business teams. Translating design decisions into business language.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can explain basic design choices and present work to immediate team. Responds to feedback constructively.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Presents design work confidently to cross-functional stakeholders. Articulates the 'why' behind design decisions in terms of user needs and business impact. Navigates conflicting stakeholder feedback effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Presents design strategy to executive leadership. Drives alignment across multiple teams and senior stakeholders. Translates design outcomes into business metrics and ROI language. Influences product strategy through design storytelling.

skill_library.name: Designing for Complex Systems
skill_library.description: Creating intuitive user experiences for inherently complex products — enterprise SaaS, data-heavy platforms, cybersecurity tools, financial systems, and multi-layered workflows. The ability to simplify complexity without losing necessary depth.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow established patterns for complex interfaces. Understands the challenge of balancing simplicity with feature depth.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs clear, usable interfaces for complex workflows and data-intensive products. Effectively uses progressive disclosure, contextual information, and smart defaults to manage complexity.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Expert at taming complexity — transforms sophisticated enterprise workflows and technical systems into elegant, intuitive experiences. Defines design frameworks for complex product domains. Balances power-user depth with approachability.

skill_library.name: Conversion & Growth Design
skill_library.description: Designing user experiences optimized for conversion, retention, and growth metrics. Includes CRO (conversion rate optimization), A/B test design, funnel optimization, pricing page design, onboarding flows, and data-driven design iteration.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic conversion principles — clear CTAs, reduced friction, trust signals. Aware of A/B testing concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs conversion-optimized experiences based on data and user psychology. Creates effective A/B test variants. Optimizes funnels, onboarding flows, and monetization surfaces. Makes data-informed design decisions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads growth-focused design strategy. Builds experimentation frameworks for rapid, consistent testing. Deeply understands user psychology and behavioral economics as applied to conversion. Drives measurable business impact through design optimization.

skill_library.name: Design-to-Development Handoff
skill_library.description: Preparing and delivering design specifications to engineering teams for implementation. Includes creating detailed specs, annotating edge cases, defining responsive behavior, QA-ing implementation against designs, and collaborating with developers throughout the build process.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can prepare basic design specs with measurements and assets. Understands the concept of developer handoff.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Creates comprehensive design specifications including responsive behavior, edge cases, error states, and interaction details. Reviews implementation and provides clear feedback. Understands basic front-end constraints.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds efficient handoff workflows and systems. Understands front-end architecture well enough to design for implementation efficiency. Uses design-to-code tools to accelerate handoff. Ensures design-to-code fidelity at scale.

skill_library.name: Cross-Functional Design Collaboration
skill_library.description: Working effectively with product managers, engineers, data analysts, researchers, marketers, and other stakeholders throughout the design process. Includes facilitating workshops, participating in agile ceremonies, and integrating design into cross-functional workflows.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in cross-functional meetings and incorporates feedback from other disciplines. Communicates design progress clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Drives productive collaboration with PM, engineering, and data teams. Facilitates design workshops and co-creation sessions. Navigates competing priorities across functions.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Shapes cross-functional processes and team dynamics. Partners with leadership across functions to drive product strategy. Builds bridges between design, engineering, and business organizations.

skill_library.name: Design Portfolio & Case Studies
skill_library.description: Creating and maintaining a professional design portfolio that effectively communicates design process, thinking, and outcomes. Includes writing case studies, documenting design decisions, and presenting work in a compelling narrative format.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Has a basic portfolio showing completed design projects with descriptions of the work.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Maintains a polished portfolio with detailed case studies showing process, reasoning, and measurable outcomes. Tailors portfolio presentation for different audiences.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Portfolio demonstrates strategic impact and leadership. Case studies show how design drove business outcomes. Uses portfolio as a tool for thought leadership and team recruiting.

skill_library.name: Outbound Prospecting & Lead Generation
skill_library.description: Generating new business pipeline through proactive outreach — cold calling, email sequences, LinkedIn engagement, and social selling. Identifying target accounts, researching decision-makers, and initiating conversations that create qualified opportunities.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can execute basic cold outreach using scripts and templates. Understands the concept of prospecting sequences and lead qualification criteria.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds and manages multi-channel outbound campaigns independently. Personalizes messaging based on account research. Consistently meets activity and pipeline targets. Uses AI tools to enhance outreach quality and volume.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs outbound strategy for teams including targeting, messaging, sequencing, and channel optimization. Builds AI-powered prospecting workflows. Coaches others on effective prospecting techniques and continuously optimizes conversion rates.

skill_library.name: Lead Qualification & Discovery
skill_library.description: Assessing prospect fit and readiness through structured discovery conversations. Applying frameworks (BANT, MEDDPICC) to determine budget, authority, need, and timeline. Converting initial interest into qualified, sales-ready opportunities.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can conduct basic qualification calls using a script. Understands BANT criteria and can assess basic fit.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads structured discovery conversations independently. Uncovers pain points, identifies stakeholders, and assesses deal potential. Applies qualification frameworks consistently and produces quality handoff briefs for sales.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs qualification processes and frameworks for teams. Trains others on discovery methodology. Optimizes qualification criteria based on win/loss analysis and pipeline data. Adapts approach for complex enterprise buying committees.

skill_library.name: CRM Management & Sales Operations
skill_library.description: Managing customer and prospect data in CRM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot). Maintaining accurate pipeline records, tracking activities, managing deal stages, and producing forecasts and reports that support data-driven sales decisions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can log activities, update contact records, and navigate CRM interfaces. Understands basic pipeline stages and reporting.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a well-organized pipeline with accurate data. Creates reports and dashboards. Uses CRM data to prioritize activities and inform strategy. Maintains forecasting discipline.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs CRM workflows, lead scoring models, and automation. Builds advanced reports and forecasting models. Ensures CRM discipline across teams and uses data to optimize sales processes and conversion rates.

skill_library.name: Sales Engagement & Outreach Tools
skill_library.description: Proficiency in sales engagement platforms (Outreach, Salesloft) and prospecting tools (LinkedIn Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, Clay). Building automated sequences, tracking engagement, and leveraging data to optimize outreach effectiveness.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can use basic features of sales engagement platforms — sending sequences, tracking opens and replies. Familiar with LinkedIn Sales Navigator for basic prospect research.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds and optimizes multi-step outreach sequences across channels. Uses prospecting data platforms effectively for account research and contact enrichment. Analyzes engagement metrics to improve performance.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and optimizes the sales tech stack for teams. Builds AI-enhanced prospecting workflows. Evaluates and implements new tools. Sets best practices for tool usage and trains team members.

skill_library.name: Partnership Development & Ecosystem Building
skill_library.description: Identifying, recruiting, and onboarding partners — channel partners, technology alliances, affiliates, and strategic integrators. Building partner ecosystems that create mutual value, accelerate market penetration, and drive revenue through collaborative go-to-market motions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands different partnership models (channel, technology, affiliate, strategic). Can support partner onboarding and maintain basic partner relationships.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Identifies and recruits new partners independently. Develops partner value propositions and manages the onboarding lifecycle. Builds relationships with partner stakeholders and coordinates joint activities.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and scales partner ecosystems globally. Creates partner program frameworks including tiers, incentives, and enablement. Recruits strategic partners that significantly accelerate market expansion and revenue growth.

skill_library.name: Partner Relationship Management
skill_library.description: Managing ongoing relationships with partners across commercial, technical, and operational dimensions. Ensuring partner satisfaction, maximizing mutual value, conducting business reviews, and driving partner-led revenue growth through proactive engagement.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Manages basic partner communications and coordinates logistics. Can track partner activities and maintain relationship records.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a portfolio of partners end-to-end. Conducts quarterly business reviews. Identifies upsell and expansion opportunities. Resolves partner issues and advocates for partner needs internally.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages strategic, enterprise-level partnerships with executive engagement. Develops comprehensive joint business plans. Drives significant revenue through partner channels. Influences partner strategy at the organizational level.

skill_library.name: Joint Business Planning & Co-Selling
skill_library.description: Creating and executing joint business plans with partners — aligning on goals, defining co-selling motions, building pipeline together, and tracking mutual performance. Driving revenue through collaborative go-to-market initiatives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands the concept of joint business plans and co-selling. Can support execution of existing partner plans.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops and executes joint business plans with partners. Coordinates co-selling activities and tracks pipeline contribution. Aligns internal and partner teams on shared objectives.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates comprehensive partner go-to-market strategies at scale. Builds co-selling frameworks and partner revenue models. Manages complex multi-partner initiatives and drives executive alignment across organizations.

skill_library.name: Partner Enablement & Program Management
skill_library.description: Building and managing partner enablement programs — sales training, technical positioning, certification, and playbooks that empower partners to effectively position and sell the company's solutions. Designing partner program structures with tiers, incentives, and performance metrics.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can deliver basic product training to partners. Understands partner program structures and incentive models.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops partner enablement materials including sales playbooks, technical guides, and training programs. Manages partner program operations and tracks partner performance metrics.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and scales global partner enablement programs. Creates partner program frameworks with tiered structures, certification paths, and incentive models. Builds self-service enablement platforms and measures program ROI.

skill_library.name: Commercial Negotiation & Deal Structuring
skill_library.description: Negotiating commercial terms, pricing, contracts, and partnership agreements. Structuring deals that balance company objectives with partner/customer needs. Managing the legal and financial aspects of business agreements.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic commercial terms and contract structures. Can participate in negotiation discussions with guidance.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads negotiations independently for standard deals. Structures commercial proposals with pricing models and terms. Navigates legal and financial review processes. Knows when to escalate complex terms.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Negotiates complex, high-value, multi-stakeholder deals. Structures creative commercial frameworks that drive mutual value. Manages enterprise-level contract negotiations with C-suite counterparts. Develops negotiation playbooks for teams.

skill_library.name: Market Research & Competitive Intelligence (BD)
skill_library.description: Researching markets, industries, competitors, and target accounts to identify business opportunities and inform BD strategy. Gathering intelligence on market trends, competitive landscape, and customer needs to support data-driven decision-making.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can research individual accounts and prospects using available tools. Understands basic competitive landscape.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Conducts structured market research to identify new opportunities and segments. Analyzes competitive positioning and market trends. Uses insights to prioritize outreach and inform messaging.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds comprehensive market intelligence programs. Identifies emerging market opportunities and new verticals. Influences product and GTM strategy through market insights. Presents market analysis to executive leadership.

skill_library.name: Pipeline Management & Forecasting
skill_library.description: Building, managing, and reporting on sales/BD pipeline. Tracking opportunities through stages, maintaining forecast accuracy, analyzing conversion rates, and ensuring consistent pipeline coverage to meet revenue targets.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can track opportunities in CRM and provide basic pipeline updates. Understands pipeline stages and metrics.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a well-qualified pipeline with consistent coverage ratios. Provides accurate forecasts. Analyzes pipeline velocity and conversion rates to identify bottlenecks and opportunities.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs pipeline management processes and forecasting models for teams. Builds predictable revenue engines. Coaches teams on pipeline discipline. Presents board-level pipeline and revenue forecasts.

skill_library.name: Relationship Building & Stakeholder Engagement
skill_library.description: Building and maintaining strong professional relationships with prospects, partners, customers, and internal stakeholders. Establishing trust, credibility, and rapport that drives long-term business value. Engaging stakeholders at all levels including C-suite executives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Builds rapport with peers and mid-level contacts. Maintains consistent communication and follow-through.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and decision-makers. Navigates complex organizational structures. Maintains a network of valuable professional contacts.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds and maintains executive-level relationships across organizations globally. Acts as a trusted advisor to C-suite leaders. Leverages a deep professional network to create business opportunities and strategic value.

skill_library.name: Presentation & Business Storytelling
skill_library.description: Delivering compelling presentations, product demos, and business proposals to prospects, partners, and stakeholders. Crafting persuasive narratives that connect product value to business outcomes. Adapting communication style for different audiences.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can deliver basic product presentations using prepared materials. Communicates clearly in meetings and calls.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Creates and delivers tailored presentations to senior stakeholders. Builds compelling business cases and proposals. Adapts messaging effectively for different audiences and personas.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Delivers executive-level presentations to C-suite, boards, and large audiences. Crafts strategic narratives that influence major business decisions. Represents the company at industry conferences and high-stakes customer engagements.

skill_library.name: BD/SDR Team Leadership
skill_library.description: Building, managing, and scaling business development or sales development teams. Includes hiring, coaching, performance management, quota-setting, compensation design, and building career development paths for BD/SDR professionals.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Mentors peers informally. Can support onboarding of new team members.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a small BD/SDR team. Conducts coaching sessions, pipeline reviews, and 1:1s. Hires and onboards new team members. Sets and tracks team performance metrics.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds and scales global BD/SDR organizations. Designs compensation plans, performance management systems, and career frameworks. Recruits and develops top talent. Creates high-performance team cultures with clear accountability.

skill_library.name: Channel & Alliance Strategy
skill_library.description: Designing and executing channel sales strategies and technology alliance programs. Building indirect sales motions through partners, distributors, and resellers. Understanding channel economics, partner program design, and ecosystem dynamics.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands channel sales models and partner program basics. Can support channel activities and partner coordination.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops and executes channel strategies for specific regions or segments. Recruits and activates channel partners. Manages partner pipeline and tracks channel contribution to revenue.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs global channel and alliance strategies. Creates partner program frameworks with tiered structures and incentives. Builds strategic alliances with major technology vendors. Drives significant revenue through indirect sales channels.

skill_library.name: AI-Powered Sales & Prospecting
skill_library.description: Leveraging AI tools and automation for sales development workflows — personalized outbound at scale, prospect research enrichment, automated sequencing, intent-based targeting, and AI-assisted lead scoring. Understanding how to integrate AI into the BD process effectively.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Aware of AI sales tools and has experimented with basic AI-assisted outreach or research.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Actively uses AI tools in daily BD workflow — personalized email generation, prospect enrichment, intent-based targeting. Understands how to combine AI outputs with human judgment for effective outreach.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds AI-powered BD pipelines and workflows. Evaluates and implements AI tools for teams. Designs AI-enhanced prospecting strategies that significantly increase pipeline quality and efficiency. Establishes best practices for responsible AI use in sales.

skill_library.name: Go-to-Market Strategy
skill_library.description: Developing and executing go-to-market plans for products, services, or market segments. Defining target markets, positioning, pricing, channel strategy, and launch execution. Aligning BD activities with broader company GTM objectives.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands GTM concepts and can support execution of existing GTM plans. Aware of how BD fits into broader go-to-market strategy.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Contributes to GTM strategy development for specific segments or regions. Aligns BD activities with GTM objectives. Provides market feedback that informs positioning and messaging.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs comprehensive GTM strategies for new markets, products, or segments. Aligns cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, product, partnerships) around GTM execution. Drives market entry and expansion strategies globally.

skill_library.name: Ecosystem & Community Development
skill_library.description: Building and nurturing ecosystems of partners, investors, accelerators, community stakeholders, and industry participants. Creating programs and initiatives that establish the company as a central player in its market ecosystem.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands ecosystem dynamics and can participate in community events and partner activities.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds relationships within target ecosystems (VCs, accelerators, industry groups). Represents the company at events and develops community engagement programs.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs and scales ecosystem strategies that establish the company as a market leader. Builds deep relationships across investor, partner, and community networks. Creates scalable programs (credits, workshops, events) that drive adoption and brand authority.

skill_library.name: Client Engagement Delivery
skill_library.description: Managing consulting engagements end-to-end: scoping work, defining deliverables, managing timelines, coordinating teams, and ensuring quality delivery against client expectations. Distinct from general project management in that the 'product' is advisory work with defined engagement boundaries, client sign-off gates, and deliverable-based milestones.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports engagement logistics and tracks deliverables under supervision. Understands engagement lifecycle phases (discovery, analysis, delivery, closeout). Maintains project trackers and meeting notes.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Owns workstream delivery within an engagement. Manages scope, timeline, and deliverable quality for their workstream. Proactively identifies risks and escalates appropriately. Coordinates across client and internal teams.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads complex, multi-workstream engagements end-to-end. Manages engagement economics (pricing, utilization, margins). Navigates scope changes and client escalations. Ensures consistent quality across parallel workstreams and team members.

skill_library.name: Consulting Frameworks & Structured Analysis
skill_library.description: Applying structured problem-solving frameworks to decompose complex business problems into analyzable components. Includes hypothesis-driven analysis, MECE structuring, issue trees, root cause analysis, and benchmarking methodologies. The core analytical toolkit of management and strategy consulting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands core frameworks (MECE, issue trees, Porter's Five Forces, SWOT). Can apply a given framework to structure an analysis. Conducts benchmarking with guidance.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Selects and adapts frameworks to fit specific client problems. Develops hypothesis-driven analyses independently. Structures ambiguous problems into workable components. Conducts root-cause analysis and gap assessments.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs custom analytical frameworks for novel or complex problems. Synthesizes multiple frameworks into integrated strategic recommendations. Leads teams through structured problem-solving processes. Develops reusable methodologies and intellectual property.

skill_library.name: Client Advisory & Trusted Advisor Skills
skill_library.description: Building and maintaining trusted advisory relationships with client stakeholders from operational teams to C-suite executives. Includes understanding client needs beyond stated requirements, navigating organizational politics, managing expectations, and positioning recommendations for adoption. The relationship-building core of consulting.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Participates in client meetings professionally. Builds rapport with day-to-day client contacts. Communicates clearly and follows through on commitments. Understands the difference between consulting advice and operational support.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages ongoing client relationships at the manager/director level. Proactively identifies unstated client needs and pain points. Navigates organizational dynamics to position recommendations effectively. Handles difficult conversations and manages expectations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Serves as trusted advisor to C-suite and executive leadership. Influences strategic decisions and long-term client direction. Manages complex multi-stakeholder relationships across client organizations. Develops and expands client relationships into new engagement opportunities.

skill_library.name: Proposal Development & Business Development
skill_library.description: Developing consulting proposals, responding to RFPs/RFIs, scoping engagements, and pricing advisory work. Includes identifying opportunities within existing client relationships, developing go-to-market approaches for new services, and contributing to practice growth through pipeline development.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Contributes content to proposals and pitch decks. Researches client context and competitive landscape for proposal development. Assists with formatting and assembling proposal documents.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops complete proposals including scope, approach, timeline, team composition, and pricing. Responds to RFPs/RFIs with tailored solutions. Identifies upsell and cross-sell opportunities within existing accounts. Supports client presentations and contract negotiations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Leads complex, high-value proposal efforts. Develops go-to-market strategy for new service offerings. Drives pipeline development and manages sales targets. Negotiates engagement terms and contract structures. Builds strategic partnerships and alliance relationships.

skill_library.name: Executive Communication & Presentation
skill_library.description: Communicating complex analyses, findings, and recommendations to senior stakeholders and executives in clear, compelling, and action-oriented formats. Includes developing executive-ready presentations, leading QBRs, facilitating workshops, and translating technical or analytical depth into business language that drives decisions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates clear, well-structured slides and documents. Presents analysis and findings to peer-level audiences. Writes professional reports and summaries. Adapts communication style to audience.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops and delivers executive-level presentations to VP and director-level stakeholders. Leads client workshops and facilitation sessions. Structures complex narratives into compelling storylines. Prepares and delivers Quarterly Business Reviews.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Presents strategic recommendations to C-suite audiences with confidence and executive presence. Leads high-stakes client presentations and board-level briefings. Develops thought leadership content for external publication. Represents the firm at industry conferences and events.

skill_library.name: Risk, Compliance & Internal Audit
skill_library.description: Conducting risk assessments, compliance reviews, internal audits, and control evaluations for client organizations. Includes SOX compliance, COSO framework application, regulatory compliance, GRC program design, and remediation planning. Covers both financial risk and operational risk domains.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports internal audit procedures and control testing. Documents process flows and risk matrices. Understands SOX compliance requirements and COSO framework basics. Assists with evidence gathering and audit workpapers.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads risk assessments and compliance reviews independently. Designs control frameworks and remediation plans. Conducts SOX walkthroughs and tests control effectiveness. Identifies gaps and develops actionable mitigation strategies. Partners with finance, IT, and legal teams.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise risk management programs and GRC architectures. Leads complex, multi-domain compliance engagements. Advises C-suite on risk posture and regulatory strategy. Develops new audit and compliance methodologies. Coordinates with external auditors and regulators.

skill_library.name: Financial Due Diligence & M&A Advisory
skill_library.description: Conducting financial due diligence for mergers, acquisitions, and investment processes. Includes analyzing financial statements, identifying risks and red flags, evaluating cost structures, assessing revenue quality, and generating business insights for investors, shareholders, and management teams preparing for transactions.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Assists with data collection and financial analysis for due diligence engagements. Reviews financial reports and identifies basic anomalies. Supports preparation of due diligence reports and data rooms.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads financial due diligence workstreams independently. Analyzes financial and management reports to generate business insights regarding costs, revenue, and structure. Identifies material risks and red flags. Prepares comprehensive due diligence findings for investors and stakeholders.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages end-to-end M&A advisory engagements. Advises investors and management on transaction strategy, valuation considerations, and deal structure. Leads complex multi-party transactions across sectors. Coordinates global due diligence processes across Deloitte/Big 4 network.

skill_library.name: Digital Transformation & Technology Consulting
skill_library.description: Advising clients on technology-enabled business transformation including digital strategy, technology architecture decisions, system implementation oversight, and change management. Covers ERP modernization, cloud adoption strategy, AI integration, and platform selection. Distinct from hands-on engineering in that the focus is on strategic guidance and implementation oversight rather than building.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands digital transformation concepts and common technology platforms. Supports technology assessments and system evaluation. Assists with requirements gathering and technology vendor analysis.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads technology consulting workstreams including architecture assessments, platform selection, and implementation oversight. Translates business requirements into technology solution recommendations. Manages technology change initiatives and adoption programs.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs enterprise-wide digital transformation strategies. Advises C-suite on technology investments, platform modernization, and AI adoption. Leads complex, multi-system transformation programs. Develops technology consulting practice offerings and thought leadership.

skill_library.name: Technical Discovery & Needs Analysis
skill_library.description: The ability to conduct structured technical discovery sessions with customers to uncover their current environment, pain points, integration landscape, and business requirements. Involves asking the right questions, mapping technical stacks, and identifying gaps between current state and desired outcomes.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can follow a discovery framework to gather basic technical requirements from customers. Documents findings clearly.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads discovery sessions independently, identifying unstated requirements and technical risks. Translates findings into solution recommendations.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs discovery methodologies for the team. Uncovers strategic business drivers behind technical requirements. Identifies complex multi-system dependencies and architectures.

skill_library.name: Solution Design & Architecture
skill_library.description: Designing tailored technical solutions that map product capabilities to customer business requirements. Involves creating architecture diagrams, integration plans, data flow designs, and implementation roadmaps that address specific customer environments and constraints.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can design basic integration architectures using standard product configurations. Follows established solution patterns.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs end-to-end solutions for complex customer environments involving multiple systems, APIs, and data flows. Creates architecture documentation and implementation plans.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise-grade solutions across complex multi-system landscapes. Designs reference architectures that become reusable assets. Evaluates trade-offs across performance, security, cost, and scalability.

skill_library.name: Product Demonstration & Technical Storytelling
skill_library.description: The art of delivering compelling product demonstrations that connect technical capabilities to customer business outcomes. Involves tailoring demos to specific audiences (technical vs. executive), whiteboarding solutions, and creating narrative arcs that build conviction and drive technical wins.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Delivers standard product demos following a script. Can answer basic technical questions during demos.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Tailors demos to specific customer use cases and pain points. Comfortable whiteboarding and adjusting on the fly based on audience reactions. Handles objections effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Creates demo environments and narratives that build champions. Delivers impromptu whiteboard sessions to C-level audiences. Designs demo strategies that win competitive evaluations.

skill_library.name: Proof of Concept (POC) Design & Execution
skill_library.description: Planning, executing, and managing proof-of-concept engagements that validate solution fit and demonstrate business value. Includes defining success criteria, managing timelines, coordinating resources, handling technical blockers, and driving POCs to a clear win/no-win decision.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Supports POC execution by handling specific technical tasks and documenting results. Follows POC plans created by senior team members.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs and runs POCs end-to-end, defining success criteria with customers, managing timelines, and presenting results. Converts POC wins into deal momentum.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs POC frameworks and methodologies for the SE organization. Manages complex multi-phase evaluations against competitors. Achieves consistently high POC-to-close conversion rates.

skill_library.name: Technical Onboarding & Implementation
skill_library.description: Owning the technical aspects of customer onboarding from contract signing through go-live. Involves configuring products, setting up integrations, managing data migrations, training customer teams, and ensuring customers achieve first value quickly. Focused on reducing time-to-value.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Executes onboarding tasks within established playbooks. Configures standard integrations and guides customers through setup steps.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads end-to-end onboarding for complex customers, managing multiple stakeholders and technical workstreams. Designs automated onboarding workflows and self-service resources.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds and optimizes onboarding methodologies and playbooks for the organization. Defines success metrics and continuously improves time-to-value. Handles the most complex, multi-system implementations.

skill_library.name: Integration & Middleware Platforms
skill_library.description: Hands-on experience with integration platforms, middleware, and workflow automation tools (Zapier, Make, Workato, n8n, MuleSoft) to connect systems, move data between platforms, and build automated workflows that solve customer requirements beyond native product integrations.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Can set up basic integrations using standard connectors on one or two middleware platforms. Understands webhook concepts.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds complex multi-step automated workflows across multiple systems. Troubleshoots integration failures and optimizes data flows. Works with webhooks, APIs, and custom connectors.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Architects enterprise integration strategies involving multiple middleware platforms. Designs custom connectors and complex event-driven workflows. Advises customers on integration architecture best practices.

skill_library.name: Competitive Positioning & Technical Differentiation
skill_library.description: Understanding the competitive landscape and articulating technical differentiation against specific competitors in sales situations. Involves maintaining knowledge of competitor products, positioning strengths against weaknesses, and handling competitive objections during evaluations.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands basic competitive positioning for the company's primary competitors. Can reference standard battlecards during sales conversations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Articulates detailed technical differentiation against multiple competitors. Designs POC criteria that highlight product strengths. Handles competitive objections confidently in customer meetings.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Maintains deep expertise across the competitive landscape. Creates competitive intelligence materials for the SE team. Wins head-to-head technical evaluations against strong competitors consistently.

skill_library.name: Technical Content & Enablement Creation
skill_library.description: Creating technical content that enables customers, partners, and internal teams. Includes reference architectures, best practices guides, demo applications, knowledge bases, FAQs, video tutorials, and training materials that scale the SE function's impact.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Creates basic documentation, FAQs, and knowledge base articles. Maintains existing technical content.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops reference architectures, how-to guides, and demo applications that are used by the broader SE team. Creates partner enablement materials.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Designs the content strategy for the SE organization. Creates reusable assets, templates, and automations that scale delivery. Builds community-facing content that drives brand awareness.

skill_library.name: Customer Technical Relationship Management
skill_library.description: Building and maintaining deep technical relationships with customer stakeholders across multiple levels (engineers, architects, IT leaders, executives). Involves becoming a trusted technical advisor, managing expectations, navigating organizational politics, and ensuring long-term customer technical success.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Builds rapport with technical contacts and maintains regular communication. Responds effectively to customer technical inquiries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Develops trusted advisor relationships with multiple stakeholders including technical leads and directors. Proactively identifies customer needs and expansion opportunities. Manages technical escalations smoothly.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds executive-level technical relationships with CIOs, CTOs, and VPs of Engineering. Creates technical champions who advocate internally. Navigates complex multi-stakeholder organizations and political dynamics.

skill_library.name: Solutions Engineering Team Leadership
skill_library.description: Leading and scaling a Solutions Engineering team, including hiring, mentoring, performance management, and process development. Involves building SE methodologies, defining KPIs, managing capacity across deals, and creating a culture of technical excellence and customer obsession.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Mentors junior SEs informally and contributes to hiring interviews. Helps define team processes.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Manages a team of 3-8 SEs, running regular 1:1s, setting goals, and tracking KPIs. Builds onboarding processes for new SE hires. Distributes deal support across the team effectively.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Scales SE organizations across regions and domains. Designs SE career ladders, compensation models, and operating rhythms. Builds and enables partner delivery programs. Aligns SE strategy with company revenue targets.

skill_library.name: Delivery Methodology & Process Design
skill_library.description: Designing and operationalizing repeatable delivery processes for customer implementations. Includes creating project frameworks, QA standards, launch readiness checklists, escalation paths, success metrics, and playbooks that enable consistent, high-quality delivery at scale.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Follows established delivery processes and identifies areas for improvement. Documents learnings from implementations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Designs delivery playbooks and processes for specific product lines or customer segments. Defines success metrics and tracks delivery quality.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Builds the delivery methodology for the organization from scratch. Creates frameworks that scale from high-touch to low-touch delivery. Implements tooling and automation to improve delivery efficiency and margins.

skill_library.name: Technical Sales & Business Value Articulation
skill_library.description: The ability to bridge technical capabilities and business outcomes in sales conversations. Involves building ROI models, translating features into business value, supporting pricing and scoping decisions, and partnering effectively with Account Executives to drive revenue.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Understands the connection between product features and customer business outcomes. Can support AEs with technical context during sales conversations.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Builds ROI-driven value propositions using customer data and performance metrics. Partners proactively with AEs on deal strategy. Supports commercial scoping and effort estimation.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Shapes deal strategy at the executive level. Designs commercial models for SE-delivered services. Manages delivery margins and resource allocation across a portfolio of accounts.

skill_library.name: RFP/RFI/RFQ Response Management
skill_library.description: Responding to formal customer procurement documents including Requests for Proposal (RFPs), Requests for Information (RFIs), and Requests for Quotation (RFQs). Involves interpreting requirements, coordinating input from multiple internal teams, and crafting compelling technical responses.

proficiency_levels.beginner: Contributes technical sections to RFP responses. Follows established templates and response libraries.
proficiency_levels.intermediate: Leads the technical response for standard RFPs, coordinating across teams and tailoring responses to customer requirements.
proficiency_levels.advanced: Manages complex, multi-product RFP responses for strategic accounts. Builds and maintains response libraries and templates that improve win rates.
